title: Thunderbird Mobile Feature Flag Catalog (lean / provider-agnostic)
description: Authoring + codegen source AND offline default snapshot. Dynamic targeting, percentage rollout and experiments are intentionally NOT modeled here — they are owned by the ONLINE OpenFeature provider (a managed vendor or a flagd-compatible engine). This file only declares: (1) the flag registry + codegen target, (2) each flag's baseline value, (3) the per app x channel offline overrides, and (4) the evaluation-context contract the app supplies to the provider.

$schema.description: Optional self-reference so editors can resolve this schema (e.g. 'featureflag.schema.lean.json').

version.description: Catalog version; used for the offline 'needs update' / staleness fallback.

context.description: The evaluation-context contract the app supplies to the online provider. Provider-agnostic; drives codegen of a typed context builder. NOT evaluated locally — the provider (vendor/flagd) targets on these attributes.

source.description: Where the value comes from (BuildConfig field, Build.VERSION, locale, current account...).

derived.description: Expression if derived from other attributes, e.g. '{app}-{flavour}'.

values.description: Closed set of allowed values, if any.

flags.description: The flag registry: identity, codegen target, and baseline value. One entry per flag.

type.description: Boolean only for now; the online provider may serve richer types later. To support multivariate, widen this enum and let 'default' accept the non-boolean value (or a variant key).

default.description: Baseline value used offline and whenever no per-variant override applies. Promoting a flag = set this true and drop its overrides.

target_feature.description: Gradle module path that owns the generated typed key constant, e.g. ':feature:notification:api'. Omit for legacy/core-owned keys.

time_to_promote.description: Target date to graduate or remove the flag (lifecycle hygiene).

defaults.description: Offline default snapshot. Per app, per channel, ONLY the flag keys whose value differs from the flag's 'default'. An absent/empty channel means every flag takes its 'default'. The bundled (offline) provider serves: flag.default MERGED WITH the matching app/channel override. (Keys here must exist in 'flags' — enforced by the codegen/validation tool.)

overrideMap.description: flagKey -> boolean override (value differs from the flag's 'default').
